question: What is the design configuration of NuScale's steam generator?

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the NuScale Power Module’s Reactor Coolant System (RCS), detailing its main components such as the reactor pressure vessel, steam generators, safety and ECCS valves, and associated piping and subsystems. It highlights that the steam generators are integrated within the reactor pressure vessel, forming part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, and outlines the separation between the primary and secondary sides of the system.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the NuScale pressurizer, including the RPV upper shell, baffle plate, heater bundles, and associated components. It specifies the use of low alloy steel, various grades of stainless steel, and nickel-based alloys, all in accordance with relevant BPVC Section II material specifications, to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: Each NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel contains two integral steam generators, which are part of the reactor coolant system and share the vessel’s pressure boundary. The steam generator system includes feedwater and main steam piping, thermal relief valves, and internal plenums, with the reactor pressure vessel itself forming the shell and pressure boundary for the steam generators.

edges.description: Section A provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system components, including the steam generators, while Section B gives detailed material specifications for the pressurizer, which is another component within the same system.
Section A provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system components, including the steam generators, while Section B focuses specifically on the steam generators as part of that system.

Section A describes the pressurizer components and materials within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), while Section B provides an overview of the RPV's internal configuration, explicitly listing the steam generators as integral components alongside the pressurizer, thus connecting the two sections through their shared context of RPV subsystem design.
